title: Toolkit Appendix — Standard Tools & Where to Get Them
body: The kit you pull onto boxes during an engagement. Most ship with Kali; the ones that don't are listed with their source. Stage them once at the start of an exam in a folder you serve over HTTP (python3 -m http.server 80) so you can wget them onto targets fast. This is a reference inventory, not a methodology step - grab what the situation needs. Kali already bundles linpeas/winpeas under /usr/share/peass/, SecLists under /usr/share/seclists/ (apt install seclists), and mimikatz under /usr/share/windows-resources/. Verify versions before exam day; raw GitHub URLs change.
cmd:
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# LINUX PRIVESC ENUMERATION
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# linpeas    - all-in-one Linux privesc scanner (bundled: /usr/share/peass/linpeas/)
#   https://github.com/peass-ng/PEASS-ng/releases/latest/download/linpeas.sh
# LinEnum    - classic enum script, lighter than linpeas
#   https://github.com/rebootuser/LinEnum/raw/master/LinEnum.sh
# pspy       - watch processes/cron WITHOUT root (catches scheduled jobs)
#   https://github.com/DominicBreuker/pspy/releases  (pspy64 / pspy32)
# linux-smart-enumeration (lse.sh) - tiered verbosity enum
#   https://github.com/diego-treitos/linux-smart-enumeration/raw/master/lse.sh
# GTFOBins   - reference site for abusing sudo/SUID binaries
#   https://gtfobins.github.io

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# WINDOWS PRIVESC ENUMERATION
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# winPEAS    - Windows privesc scanner (bundled: /usr/share/peass/winpeas/)
#   https://github.com/peass-ng/PEASS-ng/releases/latest  (winPEASx64.exe / .bat)
# PowerUp.ps1 - PowerShell privesc checks (part of PowerSploit)
#   https://github.com/PowerShellMafia/PowerSploit/raw/master/Privesc/PowerUp.ps1
# winPEAS .bat - no-.NET fallback when the exe won't run
# accesschk.exe - SysInternals, service/ACL checks (older v5.2 for XP-era boxes)
#   https://learn.microsoft.com/sysinternals/downloads/accesschk
# Seatbelt   - C# host survey
#   https://github.com/GhostPack/Seatbelt
# LOLBAS     - reference site for living-off-the-land Windows binaries
#   https://lolbas-project.github.io

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# ACTIVE DIRECTORY
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# mimikatz   - credential dumping / tickets (bundled: /usr/share/windows-resources/mimikatz/)
#   https://github.com/gentilkiwi/mimikatz/releases
# PowerView.ps1 - AD enumeration (PowerSploit/Recon)
#   https://github.com/PowerShellMafia/PowerSploit/raw/master/Recon/PowerView.ps1
# Rubeus     - Kerberos abuse (asreproast/kerberoast/PtT) - compile or grab a build
#   https://github.com/GhostPack/Rubeus
# BloodHound + SharpHound - AD attack-path graphing
#   https://github.com/SpecterOps/BloodHound
# Certify / Certipy - ADCS (ESC1-8) abuse
#   https://github.com/GhostPack/Certify   |   pipx install certipy-ad
# impacket   - the swiss-army suite (apt install impacket / pipx install impacket)
#   GetNPUsers, GetUserSPNs, secretsdump, psexec, wmiexec, smbexec, ticketer
# kerbrute   - fast user enum + password spray over Kerberos
#   https://github.com/ropnop/kerbrute/releases
# netexec (nxc) - successor to nxc (pipx install netexec)
#   https://github.com/Pennyw0rth/NetExec

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# WORDLISTS / BRUTE
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# SecLists   - the wordlist collection (apt install seclists -> /usr/share/seclists/)
#   https://github.com/danielmiessler/SecLists
# rockyou    - /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt  (gunzip it first on fresh Kali)
# medusa / hydra - SSH/HTTP/etc brute (bundled). medusa speaks legacy SSH crypto; hydra often can't.
# john / hashcat - offline hash cracking (bundled)

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# PIVOTING / TRANSFER / SHELLS
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# chisel     - TCP/UDP tunneling over HTTP (pivot through a foothold)
#   https://github.com/jpillora/chisel/releases
# ligolo-ng  - modern pivoting via a tun interface (cleaner than chisel for big nets)
#   https://github.com/nicocha30/ligolo-ng/releases
# socat / nc - bundled; reverse/bind shells, relays
# revshells.com - generate reverse shell one-liners for any language/listener
#   https://www.revshells.com
# python3 -m http.server 80  |  impacket-smbserver share . -smb2support  - file transfer

# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
# FRESH-KALI ONE-TIME SETUP
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════
sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y seclists impacket-scripts netexec chisel
pipx install certipy-ad 2>/dev/null
gunzip -k /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt.gz 2>/dev/null
mkdir -p ~/serve && echo 'stage linpeas/winpeas/pspy/chisel here, then: python3 -m http.server 80'
warn: Always re-pull linpeas/winPEAS/Rubeus fresh before an exam - bundled Kali copies go stale and detection/feature gaps matter. Compile Rubeus/Seatbelt yourself or use a trusted release; don't run random binaries from untrusted forks on exam infrastructure.

choices.label: Linux foothold — run the enum tools
Windows foothold — run the enum tools
Need to transfer a tool onto the target
